mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a tight labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and eventually higher interest rates, compressing equity valuations and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently generates positive 20-day returns when UNRATE is below 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a critical threshold, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance designed to cool the economy, which increases the cost of capital and negatively impacts corporate earnings, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is above 5.25%, the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly negative (p-value > 0.10) over a 5-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and retail sales indicates robust economic activity across both manufacturing and consumer sectors. This broad-based demand strength supports corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth > 0.2% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where both INDPRO and RSAFS show positive 3-month average growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in housing starts signals renewed confidence in the economy and future growth, as housing is a key cyclical sector. This positive sentiment and economic activity translate into higher S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods of housing starts recovery (HOUST 3-month average growth > 5% MoM), the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are negative on average over a 5-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, while also prompting central banks to tighten monetary policy, leading to a negative impact on S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL's 3-month annualized change exceeds 4.0%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which is a primary driver of economic growth and corporate earnings. This sustained demand directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: If the correlation between monthly RSAFS growth and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p-value > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, signifies a healthy and expanding labor market. This leads to higher consumer confidence and spending, supporting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative on average during periods when PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds 150,000 over a 3-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth indicates a strong and expanding economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate earnings and investor sentiment. This fundamental economic strength drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (p-value > 0.05) during periods where GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth exceeds 2.5% over a 7-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
